question: Who does she want present in the delivery room?
placeholder: e.g., Just us, her mom, a doula...

question: Does she want to be offered pain medication? If so, what type?
placeholder: e.g., Open to epidural after 6cm, laughing gas first, IV narcotics okay...
description: EPIDURAL: Most effective pain relief. Given after 4-6cm dilation via catheter in lower back. Numbs lower body while you stay awake. Allows rest during long labor. Trade-off: limits movement, requires IV & monitoring, rare risk of post-labor headache. NARCOTICS (IV): Given in early-to-mid labor. Injectable opioids take 15-20 min to work. Helps take edge off pain, allows some sleep. Less reliable than epidural. Trade-off: makes you drowsy, can affect baby's breathing if given close to birth. LAUGHING GAS (Nitrous Oxide): Self-administered, inhale during contractions, release between. Can use throughout labor. You stay in control. Fast, mild relief. No side effects on baby. Trade-off: provides mild relief only, must use consistently, some feel nausea. NO MEDICATION: Movement, breathing, water, massage, doula support, position changes throughout. Maintains full control & mobility. Very effective with continuous support. Trade-off: more intense experience, requires dedicated partner/support person, less predictable relief.

question: Does she want to try a natural birth with alternative comfort measures?
Does she want to move freely during labor?
Does she want to use hydrotherapy (tub/shower)?
Does she want the baby continuously monitored?
Does she want delayed cord clamping?
Any special requests for a C-section if needed?
placeholder: e.g., Delayed cord clamping, skin to skin, dad holds baby...
